title: 2021 Super League Grand Final

{{Short description|Rugby league final match}} {{Multiple issues| {{Update|date=October 2021}} {{refimprove|date=November 2025}} }} {{Infobox rugby league football match |year = 2021 |title = Super League Grand Final |image = |imagesize = |alt = |home = [[Catalans Dragons]] |away = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] |home_abbr = CAT {{leagueicon|Catalans|12}} |away_abbr = STH {{leagueicon|st Helens|12}} |home_half1 = 4 |home_half2 = 6 |home_total = 10 |away_half1 = 6 |away_half2 = 6 |away_total = 12 |date = 9 October 2021 |location = [[Manchester]] |MoM_title = [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] |MoM = {{flagicon|Fiji|12}} [[Kevin Naiqama]]
({{leagueicon|St helens|13}} [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]) |anthem_title = [[And did those feet in ancient time|Jerusalem]] |anthem = [[Laura Wright (singer) |Laura Wright]] |referee = Liam Moore |attendance = 45,177 |stadium = [[Old Trafford]] |network = {{flagicon|UK}} [[Sky Sports]]
{{flagicon|AUS}} [[Fox League]] |commentators = |commentators2= |commentators3= |tournaments = [[Super League Grand Final]] |last = [[2020 Super League Grand Final|2020]] |next = [[2022 Super League Grand Final|2022]] }} The '''2021 Super League Grand Final''' was the '''24th official Grand Final''' and championship-deciding game of [[Super League XXVI]]. The game was contested between [[Catalans Dragons]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]]. The game concluded by St Helens edging out Catalans by two points.{{Cite web |last=Carter |first=Paul |last2=Fiveash |first2=Greg |last3=Lucantonio |first3=AJ |title=Rugby League Project |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/45468 |url-status=live |access-date=6 November 2025}} ==Background== The two finalists finished first and second in the regular season with Catalans winning 19 of their 23 games and St Helens 16 out of 21. These league positions earned both clubs the chance to go to the second (semi-final) round of the play-offs and home advantage in their semi-final matches. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Position|Pos}} !style="width:20%;"|Team !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Played|Pld}} !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Won|W}} !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Drawn|D}} !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Lost|L}} !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Points for|PF}} !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Points against|PA}} !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Points scored percentage|PP}} !style="width:5%;"|{{abbr|2=Win percentage|WPCT}} |- |1 |style="text-align: left;"|{{leagueicon|Catalans|12}} Catalans Dragons |23 |19 |0 |4 |688 |398 |172.9 |'''82.61''' |- |2 |style="text-align: left;"|{{leagueicon|st helens|12}} St Helens |21 |16 |0 |5 |548 |229 |239.3 |'''76.19''' |} ===Route to the final=== ====Catalans Dragons==== Catalans finished first in the regular season, to claim their first [[League Leaders Shield]] since entering the competition in [[Super League V|2000]]. In their semi-final, they played [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]], the lowest ranked winning team from the elimination finals. Catalans won the match 28–10, with tries from [[Benjamin Garcia]], [[Josh Drinkwater]], [[Arthur Mourgue]], [[Fouad Yaha]] and [[Joe Chan]] to reach their first ever grand final, and becoming the first non English team in super league era to do so. {| class="wikitable plainrowheaders" style="text-align:center;margin-left:1em;" !width="25" | Round !width="150" | Opposition !width="60" | Score |- !scope=row style="text-align:center"| Preliminary Final | {{leagueicon|Hull KR|16}} [[Hull KR]] (H) |bgcolor=DDFFDD| 28-10 |- | colspan="3" style="background-color:white;"| '''Key:''' (H) = Home venue; (A) = Away venue; (N) = [[Home advantage#Neutral venues|Neutral venue]]. |} ====St Helens==== Reigning and defending champions St Helens finished second in the regular season and faced [[Leeds Rhinos]] (the highest ranked team to win an elimination final), in their semi-final. St Helens won the match 36–8, to reach the grand final for a 3rd consecutive year, a record 13th time. {| class="wikitable plainrowheaders" style="text-align:center;margin-left:1em;" !width="25" | Round !width="150" | Opposition !width="60" | Score |- !scope=row style="text-align:center"| Preliminary Final | {{leagueicon|Leeds|16}} [[Leeds Rhinos]] (H) |bgcolor=DDFFDD| 36-8 |- | colspan="3" style="background-color:white;"| '''Key:''' (H) = Home venue; (A) = Away venue; (N) = [[Home advantage#Neutral venues|Neutral venue]]. |} == Match details ==
